name: Besan Barfee
recipeIngredient: 180 grams Chick pea flour (also called besan)
1 tablespoon Almonds, blanched and coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the chick pea flour.
2. Heat oil in a heavy frying pan, wok, or saute pan over medium flame.
3. Add the sifted chick pea flour to the hot oil and stir until it turns a darker shade and is cooked (it should taste fried, not raw). Set aside to cool.
4. Make syrup by combining 1 cup of water with the sugar in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then simmer gently for about 20 minutes or until the syrup reaches a one-thread consistency. Test by dipping a wooden spoon; it should coat slightly and form a sticky thread when separated from the fingers.
5. Pour the hot syrup over the cooled chick pea flour mixture. Add cardamom seeds and nuts, mixing well. Stir until the mixture begins to harden slightly but remains pourable.
6. Transfer the mixture into a 9-inch square cake tin, pressing it evenly and tilting the pan so it flows to the edges. Allow to cool completely.
7. Cut into cubes (3/4 inch). Wrap tightly in aluminum foil and store in a plastic container; besan barfee freezes well.
MAKES: 144 little cubes
